title: Best Favorite Moments of Quality Animation in NGE
display_title: Best Favorite Moments of Quality Animation in NGE
wikitext:
[]: Best/Favorite Moments of Quality Animation in NGE             Quote Post by movieartman » Sun Apr 27, 2014 11:54 pm Ok more and more Sadly I here people bashing the animation in the original series, I really don't agree for the most part. And I also disagree with the idea that it was good then just got worse and only worse. I think the first episode was strong and then it quickly got poorer as I think episode 6 was probably some of the weakest in places. But then immediatly afterwards 8 IMO was very strong with the bouncing from battleship to battleship as all of that sequence. And the Leliel, bardiel and zeruel scenes where extremely strong. I think the absolute peak of the animation was the entire uncut Armisael sequence. From the angel tower and the morphing the angel and unit 0 go thru, to even simplier things when unit 01 is launched mabye it's just me but it seems more detailed and also more massive and intemidating than usual. And the subtly of its arms swaying from the released restraints are nice. And even just the glowing tentacles movement is strong https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=XjyBv7PT7yI So my full question is what is your favorite/and what in your opinion is the strongest moments of quality animation in the original series episodes #1-26 (EOE is exempt!)

[]:             Quote Post by Princess Asuka » Mon Apr 28, 2014 12:06 am Episode 4 was well animated and well written.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Mon Apr 28, 2014 12:45 am I'm probably the only one who liked the contrast-rich style from ep 16.

[]:             Quote Post by movieartman » Mon Apr 28, 2014 12:50 am Kendrix wrote: I'm probably the only one who liked the contrast-rich style from ep 16.

[]: Re: Best/Favorite Moments of Quality Animation in NGE             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Mon Apr 28, 2014 1:12 am movieartman wrote: But then immediately afterwards 6 IMO was very strong with the bouncing from battleship to battleship as all of that sequence.

[]:             Quote Post by Wanderlust » Mon Apr 28, 2014 3:26 am Kendrix wrote: I'm probably the only one who liked the contrast-rich style from ep 16.

[]: Re: Best/Favorite Moments of Quality Animation in NGE             Quote Post by movieartman » Mon Apr 28, 2014 5:50 am Mr. Tines wrote: That was episode 8. Episode 6 was Operation Yashima. That makes it difficult to judge which episodes you are actually praising (or not).

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Mon Apr 28, 2014 7:15 am Evangelion's animation quality is quite exquisite given the production conditions and the meagre budgeting. Nevertheless the main reason why Eva is still such a aesthetically rich and stunning work of course is mostly result of Anno's superb directing and exceptional deftness in allocation of frames where they matter. Anno does with framing, editing and mise en scene something many major budget productions have hard time competing with despite MUCH larger resources and number of frames vastly superior. It's also easy to see how much worse Eva's cheapness would've been in hands of lesser director. Just take a look at Eva's contemporaries like Macross 7 which operated on comparable if not slightly bigger budget even with really unedifying results. Something like episode 12 is great example of this. It is very low-key in terms of actual animation for majority of it only to explode at the end with still impressive Sahaquiel sequence for which cels and sakuga animators were reserved. Rest of the episode comes off just as well because of the skillful direction more than any great feats of animation which do not really exist. Anyway, there is some great animation in Evangelion here and there. All Production I.G. episodes are just really solid throughout (making it no brainer GAINAX would team up with I.G. for films) and GAINAX's A-list sakuga legend animators dash out goodness here and there. In particular work of Takeshi Honda, Yoh Yoshinari and the like must be singled out. If you ever look up relevant MADs it quickly becomes obvious it's this handful of very talented animators who are responsible for almost all of Eva's best looking cuts from episodes 1-2 to climax of episode 12 to berserking Eva-01 in episode 19. (Well, I think Mitsuo Iso only worked in ep 1 in tv series...) Rather than episodes (Tatsunoko B-team episodes like 4 and 6 tend to be the worst in terms of art quality though animation is always competent at least) it is much easier to pick certain parts: - To cheat my own criterion immeaditly most of episodes 1 and 2 count. Sachiel fight is full of great animation, Mitsuo Iso's work on initial fight between JSSDF helicopters (or whatever they were) and Sachiel and ensuing explosions is great and showcased some of his really original, influential effects animation. Misato's bubbly movements, hyperactivity and GAINAXing is also great throughout. Keeping up the general Eva tradition of reserving animation resources for Angel fights those pretty much never look bad even from here-on but the work in these episodes is truly exquisite nevertheless. - For good, low-key character animation NGE ep 5 is fine exhibit. Junichi Sato storyboarded scenes like the dinner with Ritsuko and Shinji watching Gendo and Rei interact are good and full of expressive nuance. Of course the greatness of these scenes have commonly far more to do with Anno's directing (e.g. the care he paid to eyes and lines of sight) than any KyoAni tier character animation magic but eh, deserves a nod. - NGE ep 5 also has the fantastic Eva-00 freakout flashback. That whole scene is excellently animated. - A lot of the Yoshinari "bank" animation for the bridge/SHOGOKI LIFT OFF stuff and the like are also good character animation. Given it's recycled it better be! - Episode 8 in general, Eva-02 action in particular and Asuka leaping from ship to ship in particular particular! That's one very well animated episode with fun details in character animation and strong action. - Training montage in episode 9 and the "Dance" fight are both superb. I forget who did latter but montage was pretty much singlehandedly animated by Masayuki. - No real standout parts or anything but I think Ghibli's throughoughly solid work in ep 11 deserves a nod. The character designs deviate a bit from standard (see Wanderlust's "derp" comment) but that doesn't change the fact said designs are extremely consinstent in-episode and animation work is very solid with nary a weak cut. Eva was pretty terrible at keeping character models consistent anyway thanks to rampant outsourcing and out of those episodes I think ep 17's bug people are the worst. - Climax of Action Arc aka episode 12. You can tell they put some strong effort into this. Shots of running and jumping Evas and Sahaquiel's descent are still bloody sweet. 2.0 of course outdid tv series take on the scene but I'm still very much impressed by the original sakuga work here. - Again, not necessarily episode heavy on animation pyrotechnics (the story of episode doesn't even allow for those) but Production I.G.'s first work/ep 13 is good. - Ep 16 is great and the Film Noirish strong contrasts and shadow make it a joy even outside Anno's experimental and fucking fantastic directing in mindfuck sequences. However in terms of animation I don't think there's anything distinguishing about the episode. With one exception: cuts of Leliel's gory death and Eva-01's symbolic rebirth are very powerful even today (but I don't single out whole scene for animation: great directing but a lot stills and minimally animated shots of talking heads whenever the focus isn't on Leliel bursting) - Episode 18 aka the second Production I.G. episode. Even with some cribbed animation from ep 2 the Bardiel fight is still visceral and glorious. - Pretty much all of episode 19 but, as always with NGE, Zeruel fight got all the best footage. Probably the best action animation in whole series (eps 1-2 perhaps excluded) and then there's Eva-01 freakout and the animalistic berserk stomping around. THAT is A-tier 90s tv sakuga. - I feel a bit leery about including these since they were animated for feature (Death & Rebirth) so it kinda goes without saying the sakuga in these clips is among the best in television series... ANYWAY. Almost all new DC cuts in eps 22-24 count. New Asuka scenes in ep 22 are all very well animated and Armisael sequence is truly superb through and through - and then it culminates in that fucking Angel Tower... but uhh yeah, obviously all this footage is fantastic but I'm not sure if it's entirely fair to include it. - Eva-01 vs Eva-02 in ep 24. - The morphing shapes and the like in ep 26 are great and were IIRC co-animated entirely by Anno and Masayuki. ******************* That's kinda it for stuff that stands out. For most part animation in NGE gets the job done while it is Anno's directing that elevates series into the aesthetic stratosphere it reaches. Contemporary Escaflowne was much better animated by episode to episode basis but aesthetically it never stands up against average Eva episode (except in music department where Escaflowne stomps all over NGE erry single episode). End of Evangelion is fantastically animated through and through. In particular the whole MPE fight sequence stands even today as THE exhibit of Mitsuo Iso's insane skill and as probably the best mecha action ever put on screen. I think only with Q Rebuild finally matched and arguably bettered EoE in overall animation quality.

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Mon Apr 28, 2014 8:57 am Never gotten behind the idea that NGE is "badly animated". It's gorgeous animation & has some of the best direction I've ever seen in the animated medium! It may not have the -usually awful - flourishes a lot of other Japanese animus has but it's not some kind of constant eye sore. As Xard says, while NGE had a small budget it was Anno's expertise as a director & his understanding of when to go big with the animation & when to skimp for dramatic effect that helped the show the classic it is today. Favorite animation? As some others have said - I LOVE the stark look to Episode 16. The rich black shadows do so much for me & of course Episode 19 is just the best.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Mon Apr 28, 2014 8:59 am Xard wrote: End of Evangelion is fantastically animated through and through. In particular the whole MPE fight sequence stands even today as THE exhibit of Mitsuo Iso's insane skill and as probably the best mecha action ever put on screen.

[]:             Quote Post by Rei IV » Mon Apr 28, 2014 9:32 am Xard, you really should get to writing your own blog or making a website. Your interesting, informative posts as well as your equally interesting opinions and observations shouldn't be confined to wall of texts on a message board. I also agree with you on Evangelion Q in regards to the animation, that is. For some reason, I use think it was sub-par, especially when compared to the previous movies but then I realized it wasn't that it was sub par rather that the tone and story had changed, hence the animation style was revamped a bit. I also had similar sentiments about EoE but that was just bias on my part. Death & Rebirth did a great job with the Rei smile scene that it surpassed the original, but that's a given.

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Mon Apr 28, 2014 9:38 am Pretty sure the timing on this gif is a bit too fast but meh Also Mitsuo Iso  SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Mon Apr 28, 2014 9:39 am @ Rei IV: Operation US was one of the coolest thing ever seen in animation! the Wunder spinning like a carousel to get rid of the NS was very narmy though, but it get a pass because it comes from Misato's brain, and she's the resident specialist for insane plans that nonetheless works! The fight in Lilith's Chamber can barely be called a fight, since it consisted on Asuka charging Shinji's RS Hoper's ATF, the later trying to avoiding her attacks, and Mari trolling an pinned down Rei Q. Damn and I wanted to see Asuka and Shinji really going on it against each other!

[]:             Quote Post by Rei IV » Mon Apr 28, 2014 9:42 am TMBounty_Hunter's post is a win.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Mon Apr 28, 2014 9:52 am TMBounty_Hunter wrote:

[]:             Quote Post by Dataprime » Mon Apr 28, 2014 11:43 am I always thought that the Rei smiling scene was beautifully animated.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Mon Apr 28, 2014 11:57 am HOTLINK REMOVED

[]:             Quote Post by ApolloIVtv » Tue Apr 29, 2014 2:04 am I pretty much agree with what most people have to say on here, but what I can say that's different though is that I really like ep 15, mainly because that's when I think the characters were the best modeled and animated. They just look normal and like themselves, as well as resemble closer to the look of Sadamoto's designs, the newer eva animations (DC eps and Death), and End of Eva, unlike let's say the Ghibli episodes.

[]:             Quote Post by Wanderlust » Tue Apr 29, 2014 3:54 am @ Xard: Yeah, that pretty much says it all. What ultimately makes NGE a powerful and memorable visual experience is Anno's sublime direction and ability to create striking and impressive imagery with relatively limited resources, but, be that as it may, there were still quite a few fine moments that showed remarkable animating expertise from all involved. EoE being, of course, the exception, where they finally had the resources to match their talent and go all out and by god the result was glorious .

[]:             Quote Post by robersora » Tue Apr 29, 2014 6:01 am Xard , you wandering bible, teach me more. Seriously, write a blog, I'll follow it religiously. Let alone, that you have the most obscure, hard to obtain knowledge, you also have the intelligence to connect the dots and the skills to teach us weeaboos all we ever wanted to know. <3 Since Xard listed all the instances where really the animation shines and not only Anno's glorious skills, I'll gladly pick Eva-01 in berserker mode in Episode 19. I can remember, how terrified I was when I first witnessed the brute force Yui emitted. This animalistic eating is so well done! Other than that I really enjoyed the dynamic animation of Episode 8. It seems like Asuka did not only spice the apathetic cast up quite a bit but also the animation!

And I also disagree with the idea that it was good then just got worse and only worse. I think the first episode was strong and then it quickly got poorer as I think episode 6 was probably some of
